_meta.purpose: empirical willingness anchor W(t)=[TLD/n]/driveshare (artifact; not wired)
_meta.axis: OSRM seconds; TLD mapped miles->seconds via equiv_miles + Jacobian dd/dt
_meta.finding: willingness is two-scale: a robust fast head (tau_s ~7-13 min) + a heavier-than-single-exp tail. The FORM change (single-exp -> double-exp) is warranted regardless of how much the tail value is trusted.
_meta.head_trust: tau_s (fast head) is trustworthy — short-range, well-sampled, insensitive to n(t) far-field limitations.
_meta.tail_caveat: tau_l is QUALITATIVE-ONLY. n(t) is v1 UNCONSTRAINED (no 1/D_i — the very production constraint that suppresses far trips; dividing by unconstrained n mis-attributes that suppression to willingness and inflates the tail), plus finite-island truncation and the uncorrected n_Eng/n_Ire geometry ratio. Building the constrained (1/D_i) n(t) is what would firm it up.
_meta.school_shared_tau_note: per-level tau_single 235/338/1143 s. Primary vs post-primary (similar geometry) are roughly consistent; tertiary is confounded by its distinctive big-city (university) clustering already visible in its n(t) — so this is weak, not clean, evidence against the shared-tau_school design.
_meta.tuned_reference_note: STALE — these tuned tau predate the mode-sub re-tune (K far from 1); not a fair comparison, shown for reference only.
